entry.title: Washington Post: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Putin makes his imperial pretensions clear
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia likely to seize control of eastern Ukrainian region within weeks, U.S. official says
  • De-arched former Moscow McDonald’s reopens without Big Macs
  • How Shireen Abu Akleh was killed
  • Hunting the hidden dangers that lurk in Ukraine’s lakes and rivers
  • China calls U.S. a ‘bully,’ vows to ‘fight to the end’ for Taiwan
  • Infighting in Israel imperils legal safeguards from West Bank settlers
  • In Kherson, misery under Russian occupation, hope over Ukrainian gains
  • Brazil police: Items owned by missing men found in Amazon
  • 5.0 magnitude earthquake shakes eastern Turkey
  • Lawmakers from Iraq's biggest bloc resign amid impasse
  • Truck crashes fleeing Greek police: migrant killed, 17 hurt
  • 9 arrested after vicious assault on women in north China
  • Renaissance synagogues being restored in Venice's ghetto
  • Hot air brings Spain its 1st withering heat wave of year
  • UN-brokered talks on Libya elections resume in Cairo
  • Pakistan military: Soldier killed in shootout with militants
  • Men tied to hate group planned for riot, ‘confrontation’ at LGBTQ event, police say
  • Court filing offers new evidence of post-election breach in Coffee County, Ga.
  • Chris Smalls’s Amazon uprising and the fight for a second warehouse
  • 31 tied to hate group charged with planning riot near LGBTQ event in Idaho
  • Next Jan. 6 hearings to focus on how Trump’s ‘big lie’ fueled rioters
  • ‘Starfield,’ ‘Overwatch 2,’ ‘Diablo IV,’ Hideo Kojima partnership highlight Xbox Bethesda showcase
  • How the 3D-printing community worldwide is aiding Ukraine
  • Relax, This Isn’t the Future of Japanese Tourism
  • Japan’s Assertive Foreign Policy Can Start in Southeast Asia
  • It’s Time to Get Biofuels Out of Your Gas Tank
  • Politics Have Distorted Americans’ Views of the Economy
  • How the ‘Energy Shock’ Has Upended the Global Economy
  • Why Japan’s Yen Is the Weakest in 20 Years and What That Means
  • How Putin’s Spooking Japan Further Away From Pacifism
  • The sedition didn’t stop on Jan. 6. It must be stopped.
  • The Senate’s bipartisan gun deal is an encouraging first step
  • $5 gas is largely Putin’s fault
  • The San Francisco district attorney saga set back criminal justice reform
  • Personal finance class should be required in high school
  • 7 questions I want the Jan. 6 committee to answer at its upcoming hearings
  • The Saudi-backed golf rival for the PGA is a case of ‘LIV and let die’
  • Cartoon by Pia Guerra
  • A better plan than blanket student-debt forgiveness
  • It is time to refocus our police reform efforts
  • When it comes to mass shootings, acceptance is not an option
  • Is ‘stagflation’ the appropriate label?
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Pioneer, Merdeka generation seniors to receive MediSave top-ups in July
  • Top Tony awards for 'A Strange Loop,' 'Lehman Trilogy' in Broadway season after pandemic

  • Taiwan central bank set to raise policy rate as inflation hits 10-year high- Reuters poll

  • Tuivasa-Sheck, Perofeta get All Blacks call-ups, TJ left out

  • Sweden's Grant becomes first female golfer to win DP World Tour event
  • Former Wales and Lions flyhalf Bennett dies aged 73
  • Klaasen fifty puts S Africa 2-0 ahead against India
  • Care recalled as Jones names England squad for Barbarians game

  • Hamilton's back pain causes concern for Canada at Mercedes
  • Berrettini overcomes Murray to win Stuttgart title
  • Roglic cruises to victory in Criterium du Dauphine
  • Toyota win Le Mans for fifth year in a row

  • UN agency urges Iran to resume stalled nuclear talks 'now'

  • 31 Patriot Front members arrested near Idaho pride event

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Small protests in Tokyo and Sapporo against Olympic bid
  • COVID-19 cases mark 30th day of week-on-week decline in Tokyo
  • Cheering fans at soccer stadium herald a return to pre-pandemic era
  • Tamaki to seek re-election in Okinawa with focus on economy

  • Dollar hits 135 yen as U.S. yields climb ever higher
  • Japan celebrates having the most companies that are a century old
  • China accuses U.S. of trying to ‘hijack’ support in Asia
  • Seoul: N. Korea fires suspected artillery pieces into sea
  • North Korean leader reaffirms arms buildup in party meeting
  • Myanmar junta can’t beat rebels, should restore democracy--U.S. diplomat
  • Ukraine, U.S.-China relations dominate Asian security meeting
  • Zelenskyy tells Asian meeting: Stopping Russian invasion crucial for whole world
  • As fighting rages, Ukraine’s leader says troops defy Russia
  • DNA study offers third theory on origin of extinct Japanese wolf
  • Researchers find why scratching worsens chronic itching problem
  • ‘Gundam’ episode reworked into film captures ‘essence’ of series
  • VOX POPULI: Let us raise our umbrellas to a Londoner who popularized them
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 13 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-13 03:24AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Is Hong Kong a great place to live? How quality of life changed post-handover
  • Departing Hong Kong property investors haunted by same dilemma of 1997, where will the best real estate returns be found?
  • Data on asylum seekers detained in Hong Kong should be disclosed more proactively, legal expert says
  • More than 60 per cent of coronavirus-related deaths in Hong Kong were ‘likely caused by Covid-19’, figure higher among older residents
  • Global capital flows and geopolitical risks are Hong Kong’s biggest challenges for the next few years, city’s de facto central banker says
  • Chinese President Xi Jinping calls on Hongkongers to better integrate with nation’s development
  • Hongkongers around the world urged on social media to gather to mark 3rd anniversary of 2019 social unrest
  • Nearly 1 in 4 young Hongkongers from low-income families ‘lying flat’, while some feel hopeless about future, survey shows

  • Coronavirus: infections top 800 for second straight day in Hong Kong, while imported cases surpass 100 for first time
  • Carrie Lam a fighter to the end as she winds down regret-free chief executive tenure

  • Say it with aircraft carriers: why countries send in the big ships

  • China ride-hailing giant Didi to start trading on OTC market after NYSE delisting, ending an 11-month fiasco that angered Beijing

  • Taiwan’s health minister tests positive for Covid as new cases fall
  • US Navy building its ‘largest, most advanced’ nuclear sub, as Chinese fleet expands
  • To exit its zero-Covid strategy, China needs to build trust in vaccines through more transparency
  • Chinese police arrest 9 men over attack on women in Tangshan
  • What is on the agenda at the WTO’s 12th Ministerial Conference in Geneva?
  • Covid-19 cases rise in China, as ‘ferocious’ Beijing bar cluster and Inner Mongolia trigger worries
  • Netflix confirms Squid Game will return for a second season
  • India police charge 30 soldiers for killing six tribal labourers
  • Indian officials step up arrests, demolish houses to stop unrest over anti-Islam remarks

  • India’s Sonia Gandhi, opposition leader, hospitalised with Covid-related issues
  • North Korea fires suspected artillery pieces into sea, says Seoul military
  • Philippines: Tunnel drilling for first subway set to start, with Japanese machine and cash
  • Japan’s Fukushima village residents allowed to return 11 years after nuclear disaster - but do they want to?
  • US-China talks at Shangri-La Dialogue security summit give Southeast Asia ‘some comfort’, Singapore’s Defence Minister Ng Eng Hen says
  • Shangri-La Dialogue: Fiji says climate change, not conflict, is Asia’s biggest security threat
  • Why Virat Kohli, Anushka Sharma, Genelia D’Souza and other Indian A-listers are endorsing plant-based meat
  • Shangri-La Dialogue: Seoul to ‘dramatically’ enhance defence, work with Japan to deter North Korea, defence minister says
  • John Allen resigns as Brookings president amid FBI foreign lobbying probe
  • World headed for new era of nuclear rearmament, SIPRI says
  • January 6 panellists: enough evidence to indict Donald Trump
  • Emmanuel Macron’s majority in doubt after first round of French parliament vote
  • Tajikistan security services kill two ‘criminal leaders’ in restive east
  • Five teens shot in Kentucky after anti-gun rally
  • ‘No Rwanda’ protest held at UK immigration removal centre
  • US senators reach limited deal addressing gun violence after mass shootings
  • Dozens still missing two months after South Africa’s worst floods killed hundreds
  • Ukraine war: Pro-Russian separatists uphold foreigners’ death sentences
  • WTO holds high-stakes meeting: pandemic, war in Ukraine, food shortages on agenda
  • US white supremacists arrested near Idaho gay pride event, say police
  • Ukraine war: Russia’s McDonald’s renamed ahead of reopening: ‘Vkusno i tochka!’
  • Zelensky: No one knows how long war will be but Ukraine defying expectations
  • Ukraine war: Kyiv officials dispute Joe Biden’s claim Zelensky ‘didn’t want to hear’ warnings that Putin would invade
  • Hong Kong stocks slide by most in 5 weeks with regional sell-off on China lockdown and Fed tightening concerns

  • Microsoft’s new Xbox Game Pass slate boosts ‘Netflix of gaming’ with blockbuster titles
  • Future of work: How can businesses Achieve Positive-Sum Growth using AI-Driven Solutions
  • China’s US$1.3 trillion stock rebound rewards contrarian funds with three barriers challenging path to next level

  • South Korea’s 40km trail running race across Seoul’s highest mountains is a journey of exquisite peaks, parks and pain
  • 1 in 5 men dies before 65 – don’t be that guy. These Men’s Health Week tips, from taking your pulse to checking for lumps, could save your life
  • Melinda Zeman on her Hong Kong childhood, becoming an Orthodox Jew, and finding a creative outlet in jewellery design
  • Elon Musk vs Bill Gates – what are they fighting about now? The billionaire Microsoft and Tesla founders’ Twitter beef over Covid-19, bitcoin, electric cars, space travel, climate change ... and more
  • Inside Tom Cruise’s multimillion-dollar collection of cars, motorbikes and planes: the Top Gun: Maverick star owns luxury BMWs from Mission: Impossible, rare Porsches and even a vintage WWII jet
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Guardian: 13 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • New Zealand records 100,000 lightning strikes in week of storms

  • Poor countries forced to cut public spending to pay debts, campaigners say
  • Ukrainians who fled to Georgia reveal details of Russia’s ‘filtration camps’
  • Fear for democracy in Pakistan as ISI gets power over civil service

  • Ukraine fears western support will fade as media loses interest in the war
